## Summary

Adds a dedicated `/faq` Author FAQ page and links the Resources Hub Author FAQ card to it.

## Why

The deficiency roadmap calls out the FAQ rebuild as still needed: older FAQ material needs to be converted into current-scope FAQs and stripped of unsupported film/TV/screen/adaptation language.

## Changes

- Adds `/faq` as a standalone Author FAQ page.
- Organizes FAQ content into current-scope sections:
  - Evaluation basics
  - Evaluation modes
  - Reports and downloads
  - Revise and TrustedPath™
  - Privacy, security, and author control
  - Agent Readiness and Storygate Studio™
- Clarifies short-form vs long-form vs long-form multi-layer evaluations.
- Clarifies that short-form evaluations under 25,000 words use the 13 story criteria only.
- Explains Revise, A/B/C options, author custom revision, and TrustedPath™.
- Adds privacy/security FAQ answers around ownership, public visibility, training posture, and bounded external research.
- Keeps Storygate scope manuscript-first and publishing-facing.
- Explicitly states that film, TV, screenplay, and adaptation workflows are not in current public scope.
- Updates `/resources` so the Author FAQ card links to `/faq` and adds an “Open full Author FAQ” CTA.

## Scope

Public copy/page structure only.

No auth, database, storage, evaluation, scoring, report, Revise runtime, TrustedPath runtime, Storygate runtime, Agent Readiness runtime, or pipeline behavior changed.
